[aut > tun > en > Table:Short term visa for Libyan > Visa Fees 1]

VFS service charge

[aut > tun > en > Table:Short term visa for Libyan > Visa Fees 2]

Please Note:

- The Visa fee & service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

- Visa application form, please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/T8HWUOKJA9GboTqo83IGY/064a12725025f2f53c2f68f5d332e436/form-c1-2024.pdf)
- Checklist please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2q7lns0mcjqX6bIV35Hj59/cc94503cf4717b8c5485210fa5383aeb/checklist-for-schengen-visa-libyan-dec-2021.pdf).
- Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sghUUcUZbHxstKIzkZxC5/0691e0903584daf3bc7107d63206e253/checklist-libyan-medical-purpose.pdf) for Libyan applicants -Medical purpose
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are planning a short visit to Austria for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Austria is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days in any period of 180 days.
IMPORTANT 
Applications can be lodged earliest six (6) months before the planned trip.
Visas are not issued on the day of the application and may take up to 4 weeks, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation where the authorities of the represented Member State are consulted. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period for the decision may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
Holders of Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control at the point of entry and are not guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are planning a short visit to Austria for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Austria is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days in any period of 180 days.
IMPORTANT 
Applications can be lodged earliest six (6) months before the planned trip.
Visas are not issued on the day of the application and may take up to 4 weeks, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation where the authorities of the represented Member State are consulted. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period for the decision may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
Holders of Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control at the point of entry and are not guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries.

## Visa Fees

[aut > tun > en > Table:Short term visa for Libyan > Visa Fees 1]

VFS service charge

[aut > tun > en > Table:Short term visa for Libyan > Visa Fees 2]

Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in TND is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee & service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Short-term Visa Checklist- English language 
 Short-term Visa Checklist- French language 
 Short-term Visa Checklist- German language 

## To download a form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
Please note that the Austrian Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents or personal appearance for an interview. Submission of the above-mentioned documents does not guarantee that a visa is granted.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide two recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in color and: 
- Taken against a light background    (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast    against the background
- Clear share quality and with the    face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic    paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling (without    sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such    items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).
- Please stick the Photograph on the    Visa Application Form.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete. Photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the  centre.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
- Visa application form, please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/T8HWUOKJA9GboTqo83IGY/064a12725025f2f53c2f68f5d332e436/form-c1-2024.pdf)
- Checklist please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2q7lns0mcjqX6bIV35Hj59/cc94503cf4717b8c5485210fa5383aeb/checklist-for-schengen-visa-libyan-dec-2021.pdf).
- Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sghUUcUZbHxstKIzkZxC5/0691e0903584daf3bc7107d63206e253/checklist-libyan-medical-purpose.pdf) for Libyan applicants -Medical purpose

visa_types.name: Residence Permit
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Travellers, who wish to stay for more than 6 month in Austria and / or want to permanently settle in Austria, must apply for a residence permit. This kind of visa can only be applied for at the Austrian Embassy Tunis. Please visit the Embassy´s webpage  (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/fr/ambassade-dautriche-a-tunis/voyages-en-autriche/formalites-dentree/)to gather information on how to prepare an application for a national visa and how to book in for an appointment.You can also contact the Austrian Embassy via: tunis-ob@bmeia.gv.at (mailto:tunis-ob@bmeia.gv.at)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Travellers, who wish to stay for more than 6 month in Austria and / or want to permanently settle in Austria, must apply for a residence permit. This kind of visa can only be applied for at the Austrian Embassy Tunis. Please visit the Embassy´s webpage  (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/fr/ambassade-dautriche-a-tunis/voyages-en-autriche/formalites-dentree/)to gather information on how to prepare an application for a national visa and how to book in for an appointment.You can also contact the Austrian Embassy via: tunis-ob@bmeia.gv.at (mailto:tunis-ob@bmeia.gv.at)
